behavior_summary: Defines simple NPC behaviors for a 'Sorcerer' NPC: a U8P_EVILSORC::cast process that delegates spell execution to the engine spell-combat loop, a U8P_EVILSORC::look handler that returns a short descriptive bark (distinguishing dead vs alive), and a U8P_EVILSORC::use handler that reacts to player interaction by warning on a specific map and then targeting the player to initiate aggression.
U8P_EVILSORC::cast accepts a single uword parameter and calls SPELCMBT::combatSpellLoop with it to run the NPC's combat spell logic, then suspends.
U8P_EVILSORC::look checks Npc::isDead(this) and emits either 'dead Sorcerer ' or 'Sorcerer ' as a short description/bark, then suspends.

U8P_EVILSORC::use checks NPC state (dead/combat), and if alive and not already in combat performs a map-specific warning on map 0x0E ('You will not steal my spell! I will kill you first! ') and suspends; otherwise it sets Npc::setTarget(0x01, this) to target the player.
notable_control_flow: U8P_EVILSORC::cast: straightforward call to SPELCMBT::combatSpellLoop(param1) followed by suspend — no branching present.
U8P_EVILSORC::look: conditional branch on Npc::isDead(this) -> bark 'dead Sorcerer ' if true, else bark 'Sorcerer '; suspend after either branch.

U8P_EVILSORC::use: precondition checks Npc::isDead(this) and Npc::isInCombat(this). If alive and not in combat then test Npc::getMap(this) == 0x0E: if true bark a warning and suspend; after (or when not on that map) call Npc::setTarget(0x01, this).
open_questions: Exact semantic meaning of the uword parameter to U8P_EVILSORC::cast — appears to be a spell identifier but the script only forwards it to SPELCMBT::combatSpellLoop (confidence: medium).
What in-game area or context corresponds to map id 0x0E (the script treats it specially with an aggressive warning) — map identity is external data (confidence: low).
Precise semantics of Npc::setTarget(0x01, this): inferred to target the player, but the literal 0x01 behavior code/target-type mapping is not documented here (confidence: medium).

main_function.name: U8P_EVILSORC::cast
main_function.purpose: When the NPC performs a U8P_EVILSORC::cast action, delegate to the game's combat spell loop to execute the spell behavior using the provided uword parameter.
entry_conditions: Invoked as the NPC's 'U8P_EVILSORC::cast' process (processType 0x021D) when the NPC should perform a spell U8P_EVILSORC::cast.
Receives one uword parameter (param1) — likely supplied by higher-level AI or spell system.

function_map.name: U8P_EVILSORC::cast
function_map.role: Main spell-casting behavior for this NPC; forwards a uword param to combat spell engine and suspends.

function_map.name: U8P_EVILSORC::look
function_map.role: Look/examine handler: outputs short description/bark, distinguishes dead vs alive.

function_map.name: U8P_EVILSORC::use
function_map.role: Player-interaction handler: conditionally warns on map 0x0E and sets the NPC's combat target to the player when appropriate.

function_map.name: SPELCMBT::combatSpellLoop
function_map.role: Engine/system routine that implements the combat spell iteration/logic; invoked from U8P_EVILSORC::cast.

external_calls.purpose: Execute the combat spell logic for the given uword parameter (likely the spell id) and drive spell effects/iterations.
Determine whether to emit the 'dead Sorcerer ' bark or the live 'Sorcerer ' bark when examined.
Check if NPC is dead and therefore ignore or avoid aggression behavior.
Avoid re-triggering aggressive behavior if the NPC is already in combat.
Detect whether the NPC is on map 0x0E to select a special warning bark behavior.
Set the NPC's combat/attack target (called as setTarget(0x01, this)); inferred to target the player and trigger aggression.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Defines simple NPC behaviors for a 'Sorcerer' NPC: a U8P_EVILSORC::cast process that delegates spell execution to the engine spell-combat loop, a U8P_EVILSORC::look handler that returns a short descriptive bark (distinguishing dead vs alive), and a U8P_EVILSORC::use handler that reacts to player interaction by warning on a specific map and then targeting the player to initiate aggression.
